exemplar.title: How does R.F. Kuang use symbolism and an epiphany to showcase the themes of colonialism and imperialism present in âBabelâ?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does R.F. Kuang use symbolism and an epiphany to showcase the themes of colonialism and imperialism present in âBabelâ?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear, appropriate focus throughout.
Research question is sharply framed and drives every section of the essay.
The structure (introduction â thematic analysis â conclusion) aligns precisely with the investigationâs aims.
weaknesses: Methodology description lacks detail on sourceâselection criteria and evaluation procedures.
Opening paragraph spends too much space on author biography instead of connecting to the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently maintains a precise focus and an effectively communicated purpose, and the research question is clearly stated, focused, and directly tied to the discussion. Methodological completeness is the one area holding it back from a full 6; only a brief overview of sources and methods is given without criteria or deeper rationale. Overall performance sits solidly at the top of the 5â6 band but slightly below perfection, so a 5 is justified.

strengths: Source material is generally relevant and integrated into the argument.
Knowledge of plot, historical context, and postâcolonial concepts is clear and coherent.
Terminology (symbolism, epiphany, untranslatability) is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Some quotations lack page references, and there is minimal engagement with counter-evidence.
Occasional oversimplifications and a few register lapses limit precision.
Historical contextualization (e.g., Industrial Revolution) could be expanded for greater depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear understanding and mostly appropriate application of sources, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, citation errors, occasional oversimplification, and limited engagement with opposing views prevent it from reaching the upper half of that band. A mid-band score of 3 accurately reflects its strengths and minor shortcomings.

strengths: Research is clearly relevant to the research question, drawing on both primary text and secondary scholarship.
Analysis moves beyond summary into interpretive commentary (e.g., linking translation to personal betrayal).
Discussion is coherent, with a logical progression and evaluative elements in the conclusion.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally overlaps with summary and repeats earlier points rather than deepening insights.
Counter-arguments and alternative readings are sparse, limiting evaluative depth.
Some claims (e.g., silver bars and technological advance) lack direct supporting evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking performance aligns with the middle of the 4â6 band. The student achieves good relevance and coherent argumentation, but minor inconsistencies in depth and a lack of alternative perspectives prevent an upper-band score. A score of 4 reflects solid analytic ability tempered by areas in need of further critical depth.

strengths: All required sections are present, with logical headings and subheadings guiding the reader.
The layout (title page, table of contents, pagination, works-cited) is correctly applied.
Headings clearly mark shifts between thematic analyses, aiding readability.
weaknesses: The table of contents lacks page numbers in one column, reducing usability.
Minor formatting glitches (uneven line breaks, ambiguous pronoun references) interrupt polish.
Stylistic symbols (e.g., overline notation) appear inconsistently.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is strong overall, with complete structure and correctly applied layout, but a few formatting omissions and usability issues prevent a perfect score. Performance sits solidly in the 3â4 band but just below seamless execution, warranting a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââHow does R. F. Kuang use symbolism and an epiphany to showcase the themes of colonialism and imperialism present in Babel?ââis clearly phrased, sharply delimited to two narrative devices, and directly drives all subsequent discussion. Every analytical section relates back to this RQ, demonstrating strong focus. Minor overlap between the terms âcolonialismâ and âimperialismâ does not materially weaken clarity, so performance aligns with the upper-band expectations.

The essay moves beyond description into interpretive commentaryâfor instance, linking translation to personal betrayal and analysing Robinâs Canton experience as a turning point. Arguments are generally coherent and supported by quotations. However, analysis sometimes drifts into summary and occasionally lacks depth when connecting secondary criticism to new insights, creating minor inconsistencies. This corresponds to a solid âgoodâ analysis, mid-band 4â6.

Quotations from Babel are frequent and generally well-integrated; secondary voices (Bosma, Stein, Kohlke) are introduced to support points about translation and silver bars. While quantity is modest, each citation is relevant and connected to argumentation. Occasional paraphrase without page numbers and minimal engagement with counter-evidence prevent a higher score, but overall application is mostly appropriate, fitting the 3â4 range.

The essay includes a title page segment with word count, table of contents, consistent heading hierarchy, and a works-cited list. Pagination is visible, and in-text spacing is functional. Minor formatting glitches (uneven line breaks, stylistic symbols like \overline{}) do not impede clarity, so layout fulfils the top-band descriptors.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of the novelâs plot, historical context (Opium Wars, Industrial Revolution), and post-colonial concepts. Explanations of translation as betrayal and the symbolism of silver display coherent understanding. Nonetheless, some generalisations (âlanguage is powerâ¦ in every way one looks at itâ) reveal partial oversimplification, and outside historical context is thin. The work therefore sits solidly in the âclear, partially effectiveâ 3â4 band.

A clear introduction, sequential thematic chapters, and a conclusion create a logical and complete structure. Headings, sub-headings, and a table of contents enhance navigability. All required EE sections are present, and the argument builds coherently, meeting the highest presentation band for structure.

Evidence is drawn from primary and secondary texts, and each section links chosen examples to the research question, indicating good relevance. Source range is modest (three secondary articles plus the novel) but sufficient for a literary EE. Some claims (e.g., impact of silver on âmass productionâ) could be better substantiated, yet overall research quality is good with clear pertinence, satisfying the 4â6 band at roughly midpoint.

The student states a precise focusâexamining how symbolism and epiphany reveal colonialism and oppression in Kuangâs Babelâand maintains this focus throughout the essay. Each major section (translation, silver bars, Tower of Babel, epiphany) is explicitly tied back to that purpose, showing clear alignment between chosen topic and the analytical goal. Although stylistic polishing could improve concision, the purpose remains consistently appropriate and effectively communicated, matching the descriptors of the 5â6 band.

The discussion maintains a logical progression (symbolism sections â epiphany â conclusion) and articulates a reasoned stance on how Kuang exposes colonialism. The student acknowledges limitations and raises open questions about post-colonial futures, evidencing evaluative thought. Still, counter-arguments and critical weighing of alternative readings are sparse, so the argument, while effective, is not fully developed to the highest standardâappropriate for the 4â6 band.

A brief methodology is included (section 1.2) describing use of textual quotations and secondary scholarly commentary. The selection of a primary text and several academic sources shows informed choices, and the essay structure (introduction â thematic sections â conclusion) is logical. However, details about source-evaluation procedures, criteria for inclusion, or alternative methods are limited, so completeness is partial rather than full, matching the middle band.

Key literary termsâsymbolism, epiphany, narrative technique, colonialismâare used accurately and consistently. The student also employs discipline-specific phrases (âact of translation,â âuntranslatability,â âindustrial revolutionâ) correctly. Some lapses in register (e.g., conversational asides) and minor spelling errors (âfragilityâ rendered as âfrugalityâ) limit precision, keeping performance at the upper end of the middle band rather than the top.

annotations.comment: The paragraph linking silver bars to the Industrial Revolution and exploitation is a strong analytical connection.
The pronoun âitâ in â...that partake in itâ is ambiguous; refine phrasing to specify the referent.
The biblical quote is missing proper closing punctuation; ensure full quotation marks and correct punctuation.
Drawing parallels between the biblical story and Kuangâs Tower of Babel is an insightful approach that deepens thematic understanding.
The long discussion in this section includes repetition of earlier points; consider consolidating similar ideas for clarity.
Section heading â3. The epiphanyâ clearly signals shift to narrative technique analysis, maintaining logical structure.
The student articulates a precise investigation topic that clearly frames the analysis around symbolism and narrative techniques in Babel, demonstrating strong focus and purpose.
The research question is concise, well-focused on symbolism and epiphany, and directly related to themes of colonialism and imperialism.
Including a word count meets IB formatting requirements, showing attention to formal guidelines.
The table of contents provides a clear roadmap of sections and subsections, aiding navigation of the essayâs structure.
The second column in the TOC is left blank; adding page numbers will improve usability and conform to presentation standards.
The opening paragraph focuses heavily on author biography rather than directly linking to the research question; consider condensing biographical details and tying them to thematic analysis.
This extensive plot summary lacks analytical framing; shift emphasis to how these events illustrate symbolism and epiphany.
Footnote marker â1â appears without clear linkage to a reference list; ensure consistent citation formatting.
The worthiness section establishes relevance effectively by linking colonial themes to contemporary significance.
The methodology description is broad and does not specify criteria for source selection or evaluation; detail your methodological choices more explicitly.
The main section headings (e.g., â2. Symbolismâ) clearly structure the analysis, aiding logical flow.
The initial discussion of translation effectively links the literary device to themes of extraction and betrayal, demonstrating strong conceptual application.
The quotation âFrankenstein vernacularâ is used without a page reference; add precise in-text citations for all quotes.
The sentence introducing Kari Stainâs paper is cut off and incomplete; ensure full integration of secondary source analysis.
Integrating Iris Bosmaâs argument on untranslatability strengthens the analysis by linking physical symbols to linguistic themes.
The claim linking silver bars to technological advance is asserted without direct evidence; add a supporting citation or data.
The analysis of Robinâs epiphany is well tied to character development and theme, showing effective interpretive depth.
The concluding section effectively synthesizes how each device supports the thesis, but could briefly discuss source limitations.
The works-cited list shows engagement with relevant secondary sources, grounding the essay in scholarly discourse.
Citation entries (e.g., Stein reference) contain formatting errors and truncations; standardize bibliographic formatting per MLA/APA.
Provide full URLs or DOIs for online sources to enhance verifiability and completeness of citations.
